texto: The principle must always rule that ideas are not born of other ideas, philosophies of other philosophies; they are a continually renewed of real historical development. The unity of history (what the idealists call unity of the spirit) is not a presupposition, but a continuously developing process.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Antonio Gramcsi sobre temas ligados a reflexao. A afirmação “The principle must always rule that ideas are not born of other ideas, philosophies of other phil…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, history, ideas, útil em momentos de reflexao.

frase_original: The principle must always rule that ideas are not born of other ideas, philosophies of other philosophies; they are a continually renewed of real historical development. The unity of history (what the idealists call unity of the spirit) is not a presupposition, but a continuously developing process.
